title: xfs hangs with assertion failed
description: Mounting external HD is fine, but extensive r/w (rsync) or attempting to unmount the device makes the kernel hang in the following sense: The device keeps mounted, no operations possible, every process accessing the device becomes unkillable. Apparently no major damage to the fs is caused. Log is attached.
patch:
commit ff18a82d96026c2fed999e2ccb73a17d8f6b0723
Author: Christoph Hellwig <hch@lst.de>
Date:   Mon Aug 10 10:40:47 2009 +0200

    [XFS] Replace spin_is_locked due to regression
    
    Replace ASSERT(spin_is_locked()) by assert_spin_locked().
    This fixes bug #13621.
    
    Author: Christoph Hellwig <hch@lst.de>
    Tested-by: Johannes Engel <jcnengel@googlemail.com>

diff --git a/fs/xfs/xfs_log.c b/fs/xfs/xfs_log.c
index 3750f04..9dbdff3 100644
--- a/fs/xfs/xfs_log.c
+++ b/fs/xfs/xfs_log.c
@@ -3180,7 +3180,7 @@ try_again:
 STATIC void
 xlog_state_want_sync(xlog_t *log, xlog_in_core_t *iclog)
 {
-	ASSERT(spin_is_locked(&log->l_icloglock));
+	assert_spin_locked(&log->l_icloglock);
 
 	if (iclog->ic_state == XLOG_STATE_ACTIVE) {
 		xlog_state_switch_iclogs(log, iclog, 0);
